title: Schema-v3 identity: a filing mints a ULID id, wires to a ULID epic, drift stays 0
regression_target: The v4 default (schema_version: 3): era-aware id allocation, story-to-ULID-epic wiring, and reconcile coverage of ULID-identified artefacts
setup: Fixture repo whose sdlc-studio/.config.yaml sets `schema_version: 3`. Seed one epic with a v3 ULID id (e.g. sdlc-studio/epics/EP-01JQK3F8-onboarding.md, Status In Progress) plus its epics/_index.md row, and an empty bugs/ with an _index.md. Skill installed.
prompt: /sdlc-studio bug create titled "login form drops the CSRF token", then link a new story to the seeded epic, then /sdlc-studio reconcile --dry-run

expected_behaviours.description: The new bug is allocated a v3 ULID id (form `BG-<8+ Crockford-base32 chars>`), NOT a v2 sequential `BG0001` - the tool reads schema_version: 3 and mints the era-correct id via artifact.py/file_finding.py
A story linked to the seeded epic resolves the FULL ULID epic id (lands in the epic's Story Breakdown), not a dash-split prefix - story-to-epic wiring works on the default schema
The id is tool-allocated (artifact.py / file_finding.py), never hand-picked, and the index row is appended by the tool rather than hand-authored
reconcile --dry-run reports drift 0 against the resulting workspace (the ULID artefacts and their index rows agree), and mutates nothing

forbidden_behaviours: Minting a v2 sequential id (BG0001 / US0001) on a schema_version: 3 project
Hand-authoring the _index.md row instead of letting the tool append it
Splitting the epic id on the first dash so the story wires to a bare `EP` and silently fails to link
Reporting drift against a workspace the tool itself produced
grading_notes: EB1 is verified by the id shape: a ULID id has a dash then a run of uppercase-base32 chars, never a 4-digit sequential number. EB2/EB3: inspect the epic file's Story Breakdown for the new story id and confirm the id was allocated by a script (transcript shows artifact.py/file_finding.py, not a literal id typed by the agent). EB4: run reconcile detect in the fixture after the session; any drift fails.
